reasoning: The historical baseline shows Artemis Accord signatories are added frequently, with approximately a dozen or more nations joining per year in the 2023-2024 period. Signings tend to cluster around major international space diplomacy events. The UN Committee on the Peaceful Uses of Outer Space (COPUOS) main session typically occurs in June (e.g., June 2024, May/June 2023, June 2022), serving as a common venue or forcing function for space agreements. Given the accelerating base rate of 6-8 months per year seeing at least one signing, and June's status as a high-activity month for space diplomacy, the probability of a signing in June is higher than average. While recent signings in April and May might suggest a temporary exhaustion of the pipeline, the US continues to actively recruit partners from a large pool of non-signatory nations. Therefore, I estimate a 68% chance that at least one country will sign the Accords in June 2025.
